title: Bounding Box Formats
concept: Understanding coordinate formats (xyxy vs xywh) and normalizing them relative to image size.

exercises.prompt: What is one reason we resize images before processing them?

options.text: To match the input requirements of the model

exercises.explain: We resize images to ensure they fit the input requirements of the model, which helps achieve better performance.

cards.title: Understanding Bounding Boxes
cards.body: Bounding boxes help us identify where objects are within an image using coordinates.

cards.title: What are Coordinate Formats?
cards.body: There are two common formats for bounding box coordinates: xyxy and xywh. Each tells us where a box is located.

cards.title: Bounding Boxes Explained
cards.body: Think of a bounding box like a frame around a picture. The xyxy format gives corners, while xywh gives the position and size.

cards.title: Coordinate Formats Example
cards.body: In xyxy (x1, y1, x2, y2): (10, 20, 50, 80) means the top-left corner is at (10, 20) and the bottom-right at (50, 80). In xywh (x, y, w, h): (10, 20, 40, 60) means it starts at (10, 20) and is 40 pixels wide and 60 pixels tall.

cards.title: Myth: One Format is Better
cards.body: Some believe xyxy is always better than xywh, but both formats have their uses depending on the task.

cards.title: Visualizing Bounding Boxes
cards.body: Here’s how the two formats look when described on an image.

exercises.prompt: Which coordinate format provides the width and height of a bounding box?
exercises.explain: The xywh format gives the width and height of the bounding box, which is useful for understanding its size.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The xyxy format specifies the size of the bounding box directly.
exercises.explain: False. The xyxy format only specifies the corners, while the size is implied.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: The xyxy format uses __________ coordinates to define a bounding box.
exercises.sentence: The xyxy format uses top-left and bottom-right coordinates to define a bounding box.
exercises.explain: The correct answer describes how the xyxy format defines the bounding box.

exercises.prompt: Which statement is AI-generated regarding bounding box formats?

options.text: Bounding boxes can only be defined by pixels.
Understanding both xyxy and xywh is important for object detection.
Any format is fine for any project without consideration.

exercises.explain: The correct statement emphasizes the importance of understanding both formats for effective object detection.

exercises.prompt: What is a key difference between xyxy and xywh formats in bounding boxes?

options.text: xyxy shows top-left and bottom-right points, while xywh shows a corner with width and height.
xywh uses only negative coordinates, while xyxy uses only positive.
xyxy is about image colors, while xywh is about pixels.
They represent the same information but are written differently.

exercises.explain: The correct answer highlights that xyxy format provides corner coordinates while xywh gives the starting point and dimensions, which is important for accurately drawing bounding boxes.
